summary:
Wide divergent roots can lead to incomplete absorption due to improper resorption phases; this increases risk of post-extraction complications like fractures at the apex. To address such problems effectively requires careful management techniques tailored specifically toward managing these unique anatomies.
To refine Agent1’s summary into two concise sentences focusing only on critical clinical points:

Wide divergent roots of primary teeth may result in unfused root segments after normal resorption, posing risks of retained fragments or apical fractures upon removal. Careful surgical technique is essential to manage these complex structures safely. 

This version maintains focus while condensing information efficiently within the specified limit.
